questions.question_summary: Calculate the correct numeric value for a specific model component from the given options
Identify the primary role of multi‑head attention in a transformer architecture
State the primary purpose or advantage of the concept being asked about
Explain the purpose of the attention mechanism in a transformer network
Identify the primary architectural difference between a gated recurrent unit (GRU) and an LSTM cell
State how the multi‑head self‑attention mechanism benefits transformer models
Calculate the total number of trainable parameters in a fully connected network with 2 inputs, 3 hidden units, and 2 outputs
Describe the main objective of a generative adversarial network (GAN)
Explain the primary purpose of the bottleneck layer in an autoencoder network
Identify which types of information can be included as input
Calculate the overall accuracy of a three‑class classifier using its confusion matrix.
Explain why a confusion matrix provides more insight than overall accuracy alone for classification evaluation.
Derive a closed‑form expression for the gradient of the specified loss function with respect to the model parameters.
Derive a closed‑form expression for the gradient of the binary‑output neuron’s loss with respect to its weights using the given update rule.
Determine the output produced by the given sequence of commands.
Determine the output tensor shapes produced by a fully connected linear layer when applied to the given input tensors xl and x2.
Calculate the output tensor shape of a Conv2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=40, kernel_size=8, padding=2 when applied to the input tensor x2.
Compute the output tensor shape of a Conv2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=32, kernel_size=16, stride=4, padding=0 when applied to the input tensor x2.
Determine the output tensor shape of a MaxPool2d layer with kernel_size=16, stride=3, padding=4 when applied to the input tensor x2.
Calculate the output tensor shape of a ConvTranspose2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=8, kernel_size=3, stride=1, padding=1, output_padding=1 when applied to the input tensor x2.

Describe the complete training process for a GRU-based text generation model, covering data preparation, model training, the role of the loss function, and how the trained model is used to generate new text.
Complete the missing parts of a PyTorch GRU class definition for a text generation model
Explain the functions of the Query, Key, and Value matrices in the attention mechanism of a Transformer
Write the mathematical expression for the attention operation using the softmax function
Describe how the self‑attention mechanism enables the neural‑dictionary functionality in a Transformer
Explain why the dot-product is scaled by the square root of the key dimension and state the shape of the final attention output.
Describe the overall structure of a graph neural network for binary chemical toxicity prediction, including how the input graph is processed and how the final classification is generated.
Propose a modification to the GNN architecture that enables it to capture global structural information of a chemical compound and discuss the benefits and potential challenges of this change.
Describe the main components of a generative adversarial network, the roles of the generator and discriminator, and how the two networks interact during training.
Identify three common architectural features in deep learning models and explain their roles in learning.
Explain mode collapse in GANs, its effect on the quality of generated samples, and suggest one method to mitigate it.
Define the loss function for the generator and the loss function for the discriminator in a GAN.
Discuss common challenges that arise during the training of deep learning models.
Compare and contrast the training processes of CNNs and Transformers, providing at least two differences and one similarity regarding architecture, model type, and dataset requirements.
Identify and briefly explain three common challenges encountered when training deep neural networks, regardless of the specific model architecture.
